executive_summary: Store delivered weekly sales of 301,811.98 inc VAT (274,726.07 ex VAT) for the week ending 20 June 2026.

Overall margin of 17.79% is under pressure; a number of high-volume departments are reporting margins significantly divergent from their scan margins, indicating stock control, waste or purchasing issues.
Total waste of 5,347.08 represents a waste percentage of 1.95% of ex-VAT sales, which is an elevated figure requiring immediate departmental review.
Fish waste of 931.05 and Provisions waste of 713.93 are the standout contributors outside of Produce (1,163.02), collectively accounting for over 50% of total store waste.

Several meat departments — Pork and Poultry — are reporting negative cost of sales figures, indicating closing stock values exceed opening stock plus purchases, which must be investigated for stock count accuracy.
Cigarettes and Deli are both reporting 100% margin at P&L level, which signals missing purchase or COGS data and these departments cannot be accurately assessed until figures are reconciled.

The Produce department is the single largest sales contributor at 26,297.16 but carries the highest absolute waste at 1,163.02, and its closing stock is not recorded, undermining the reliability of its reported 69.0% margin.
biggest_wins: Beef delivered sales of 11,743.72 with a gross profit of 11,017.23, achieving a P&L margin of 93.81% against a scan margin of 17.54% — the strong result reflects a very low COGS of 726.49 driven by favourable stock movement, though this warrants verification.

Provisions posted sales of 18,004.60 with a gross profit of 17,997.51 and a P&L margin of 99.96% against a scan margin of 21.76%, suggesting the vast majority of Provisions sales are processed without stock being drawn through the P&L — confirm that purchase data is fully captured.

Produce remains the highest-turnover department at 26,297.16 in sales, generating a gross profit of 18,144.69 at a 69.0% margin, making it the largest absolute profit contributor in the fresh trading group despite its waste challenge.
Lamb achieved a P&L margin of 79.76% on sales of 1,037.45, well ahead of its scan margin of 23.78%, indicating lean stock management and good sell-through during the week.

Fish recorded sales of 2,271.51 with a gross profit of 2,229.23 at a 98.14% P&L margin; while the scan margin is 17.75%, the negligible COGS of 42.28 suggests strong opening stock utilisation, though fish waste of 931.05 partially offsets this at store level.
biggest_risks: Pork is reporting a negative COGS of -185.70, meaning closing stock of 669.78 exceeds opening stock of 484.08 plus recorded purchases, producing an artificial margin of 116.45% — a stock count discrepancy or missing purchase invoice is the likely cause and must be corrected.

Poultry similarly shows a negative COGS of -581.42 and a margin of 107.18% on sales of 8,092.91 — closing stock of 3,336.37 against an opening of 2,754.95 with no purchases recorded is not credible for a department of this volume; purchase data must be reconciled urgently.

Fish waste of 931.05 against sales of 2,271.51 represents an effective waste-to-sales ratio of approximately 40.98% within the department — this level of spoilage is unsustainable and requires immediate review of ordering volumes, display life management and markdown procedures.

Produce waste of 1,163.02 is the highest in the store and, combined with the missing closing stock figure, means the 69.0% margin cannot be fully validated; closing stock must be entered and waste reduction strategies implemented.

Cigarettes show purchases and COGS as null with a reported margin of 100%, which is clearly a data entry or system issue — cigarettes typically operate at approximately 9.39% scan margin and the absence of cost data means this department is uncontrolled in the current P&L.

Deli is similarly reporting 100% margin with no purchases or COGS recorded against sales of 10,599.52 and a scan margin of 71.71% — Deli waste of 560.80 is also noted, and without cost data the true profitability is unknown.

Bacon and Loose SRP is the weakest performing fully-costed meat department with a P&L margin of 61.55% against a scan margin of 11.99% — while the P&L margin is high in absolute terms, the COGS of 416.27 on sales of 1,082.51 should be reviewed alongside shrinkage and markdown activity.

Provisions waste of 713.93 is disproportionately high relative to the department's COGS of 7.09, suggesting that waste is not being adequately reflected in the stock and purchasing figures and may be masking true costs.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: P&L margin reported as 100% due to missing purchase and COGS data; scan margin is 9.39% — purchasing records must be reconciled before this week closes.

Beef: P&L margin of 93.81% against scan margin of 17.54% on sales of 11,743.72 — very low COGS of 726.49 driven by stock reduction; verify stock counts are accurate and no purchase invoices are outstanding.
Lamb: P&L margin of 79.76% versus scan of 23.78% on sales of 1,037.45 — strong sell-through with COGS of 210.02; performance is positive but the gap to scan should be monitored.

Pork: Negative COGS of -185.70 producing an invalid margin of 116.45% on sales of 1,128.82 — closing stock of 669.78 cannot exceed opening plus purchases without missing data; investigate and recount.
Bacon and Loose SRP: P&L margin of 61.55% on sales of 1,082.51 — weaker than other meat lines; review markdown levels, waste (14.70 recorded) and portion control.

Poultry: Negative COGS of -581.42 and margin of 107.18% on sales of 8,092.91 — this is a high-volume department and the data anomaly has a material impact on the overall P&L; purchase invoices and stock counts must be reviewed as a priority.

Fish: P&L margin of 98.14% on sales of 2,271.51 is distorted by low COGS; however, waste of 931.05 is critically high relative to sales and must be addressed through reduced ordering and active daily markdown.

Produce: Highest sales at 26,297.16 and gross profit of 18,144.69 at 69.0% margin — closing stock is missing which affects reliability; waste of 1,163.02 is the largest in the store and a focused reduction plan is needed.
Deli: P&L margin of 100% due to absent COGS data on sales of 10,599.52; scan margin of 71.71% indicates this should be a strong department — waste of 560.80 noted; purchasing data must be entered.
Provisions: Near-100% P&L margin on sales of 18,004.60 with COGS of only 7.09 — purchase data appears largely absent; waste of 713.93 is notable and does not align with the minimal COGS recorded.

TOTAL fresh/P&L group: Combined sales of 90,825.93, gross profit of 82,038.43 at 90.32% — this blended figure is heavily distorted by missing purchase data across multiple departments and should not be used for decision-making until all data gaps are resolved.
action_plan: Reconcile all missing purchase data for Cigarettes, Deli, Poultry, Pork and Provisions with the accounts team before end of business Monday 22 June — no P&L decisions should be based on current figures for these departments.

Recount Poultry closing stock immediately; a closing stock of 3,336.37 against an opening of 2,754.95 with no purchases recorded on an 8,092.91 sales week is not credible and is materially distorting the store P&L.
Recount Pork closing stock and verify all purchase invoices have been posted; the negative COGS of -185.70 must be resolved.
Enter the missing Produce closing stock for the week — without this figure the department's 69.0% margin cannot be validated and the overall store margin calculation is incomplete.

Implement a Fish waste reduction plan with immediate effect: review daily order quantities against actual sales velocity, introduce end-of-day markdowns no later than 4pm, and set a department waste target of no more than 15% of sales.

Review Produce ordering and display rotation to address waste of 1,163.02 — introduce a daily waste log for the department manager, identify the top five wasting lines this week and reduce order quantities accordingly.

Investigate Provisions waste of 713.93 against a recorded COGS of 7.09 — either waste is being incorrectly coded to this department or purchase invoices are missing; clarify with department manager and correct coding.

Conduct a Deli waste review for the 560.80 recorded this week — identify which product categories (hot food, cold deli, bakery items) are driving the waste and adjust production schedules for the coming week.

Ensure Bacon and Loose SRP department manager reviews current stock levels, supplier invoice accuracy and markdown activity given the P&L margin of 61.55% is below expectation relative to other meat departments.

Schedule a full department manager meeting for Tuesday 23 June to review the week's P&L figures, communicate the data quality issues identified, and set individual waste and margin targets for week ending 27 June 2026.

Confirm with the system administrator that Cigarettes purchase data is being captured correctly in the P&L feed — a 100% margin on a 10,567.72 sales line is a standing reporting error that must be resolved at a system level.

Set a store-level waste reduction target for week ending 27 June of no more than 1.70% of sales (from the current 1.95%), with specific accountability assigned to Produce, Fish and Provisions department managers.
